code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural language sentence from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping predicates to phrase generators
    def alt_name(s, o): return f"also known as {o}"
    def creator(s, o): return f"was created by {o}"
    def full_name(s, o): return f"whose full name is {o}"
    def founded_by(s, o): return f"was founded by {o}"
    def key_person(s, o): return f"key person is {o}"
    def city(s, o): return f"located in {o}"
    def product(s, o): return f"produces {o}"
    def last_aired(s, o): return f"last aired on {o}"
    def starring(s, o): return f"stars {o}"
    def first_appearance(s, o): return f"first appeared in the film {o}"
    def voice(s, o): return f"voiced by {o}"
    def distributor(s, o): return f"distributed by {o}"
    def birth_place(s, o): return f"born in {o}"
    def nationality(s, o): return f"has nationality of {o}" if o != "Americans" else "is American"
    def award(s, o): return f"received the {o}"
    def broadcasted_by(s, o): return f"broadcasted by {o}"
    def first_aired(s, o): return f"first aired on {o}"
    def series(s, o): return f"part of the series {o}"
    def child(s, o): return f"has a child named {o}"

    phrase_funcs = {
        "alternativeName": alt_name,
        "creator": creator,
        "fullName": full_name,
        "foundedBy": founded_by,
        "keyPerson": key_person,
        "city": city,
        "product": product,
        "lastAired": last_aired,
        "starring": starring,
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": first_appearance,
        "voice": voice,
        "distributor": distributor,
        "birthPlace": birth_place,
        "nationality": nationality,
        "award": award,
        "broadcastedBy": broadcasted_by,
        "firstAired": first_aired,
        "series": series,
        "child": child,
    }

    # Build a set of all objects that are also subjects (for linking)
    all_subjects = set(t.subject for t in triples)
    all_objects = set(t.object for t in triples)
    linked_objects = all_objects & all_subjects  # Objects that are also subjects

    # Find the main subject (prefer comics characters)
    main_subject = None
    for t in triples:
        if "comicsCharacter" in t.subject:
            main_subject = t.subject
            break
    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Build the sentence starting from main subject
    main_triples = [t for t in triples if t.subject == main_subject]
    other_subjects = set(t.subject for t in triples if t.subject != main_subject)

    # Build main clause
    clauses = [main_subject]
    main_clauses = []

    for t in main_triples:
        func = phrase_funcs.get(t.predicate)
        if func:
            # For nationality, simplify
            if t.predicate == "nationality" and t.object == "Americans":
                main_clauses.append(f"was created by {t.object} and is American" if any(creator_triple.subject == main_subject and creator_triple.predicate == "creator" for creator_triple in main_triples) else "is American")
            else:
                main_clauses.append(func(main_subject, t.object))
        else:
            main_clauses.append(f"{t.predicate} {t.object}")

    # Add main subject predicates
    if main_clauses:
        if len(main_clauses) == 1:
            if "was created by" in main_clauses[0]:
                clauses[0] += f", {main_clauses[0]}"
            else:
                clauses[0] += f" {main_clauses[0]}"
        else:
            # Multiple clauses for main subject
            first_pred = main_clauses[0]
            if "was created by" in first_pred or "whose" in first_pred:
                clauses[0] += f", {first_pred}"
            else:
                clauses[0] += f" {first_pred}"
            for pred in main_clauses[1:]:
                if "whose" in pred:
                    clauses[0] += f", {pred}"
                else:
                    clauses[0] += f" and {pred}"

    # Add information about other subjects (creators, etc.)
    other_clauses = []
    for subj in other_subjects:
        subj_triples = [t for t in triples if t.subject == subj]
        subj_info = []
        for t in subj_triples:
            func = phrase_funcs.get(t.predicate)
            if func:
                subj_info.append(func(subj, t.object))

        if subj_info:
            # Use relative clause to connect
            if is_person(subj):
                rel_clause = f"who {subj_info[0]}"
                for info in subj_info[1:]:
                    rel_clause += f", {info}"
                other_clauses.append(rel_clause)
            else:
                rel_clause = f"which {subj_info[0]}"
                for info in subj_info[1:]:
                    rel_clause += f", {info}"
                other_clauses.append(rel_clause)

    # Insert other clauses at appropriate positions
    # Find where creator information should go
    for i, clause in enumerate(other_clauses):
        if "created by" in clause:
            # Insert after main subject
            clauses.insert(1, f", {clause}")
        else:
            clauses.append(f", {clause}")

    # Clean up the sentence
    result = " ".join(clauses)
    # Fix double spaces and ensure proper punctuation
    result = " ".join(result.split())
    if not result.endswith("."):
        result += "."

    return result

def is_person(subject: str) -> bool:
    """Check if subject is likely a person based on common patterns."""
    person_indicators = ["comicsCharacter", "Character", "actor", "voice", "creator", "born", "nationality", "award"]
    return any(indicator in subject for indicator in person_indicators)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
